title: A young cheesemaker
content_html:
16-year-old Pat Tulloch has an unusual hobby. She makes cheese on the family farm in Australia. She began by making yoghurt with her mother when she was little. Then she started watching her father's workers make cheese. When she was ten, she made some herself for the first time. 'It wasn't great,' she says, 'but the workers told me what I was doing wrong and that helped me to slowly get better.'

Pat always needs good milk for her cheese, but she doesn't have to buy it. Her mother and father keep 100 cows on their farm. Pat can just ask them when she needs more. Last year, Pat's neighbour gave her a young cow to keep and look after, but it doesn't produce milk to make cheese yet.

Pat and her family make several types of cheese. Recently they won a prize for one of them. 'It's been great for helping customers find out about us,' says Pat. 'Last month we started selling cheese in New Zealand. People there read about our prize in a food magazine. Soon we're going to do some advertisements, too.'

Pat's next idea is to post some online recipes for cooking with cheese. 'One of my favourites is cheese with eggs for breakfast. It's great! Our cheese is also lovely with pasta - I hope a restaurant might buy some one day.' But right now Pat is still at school. 'Making cheese is fun and winning a prize for it is great, but doing well in my studies matters more for now.

questions.text: Pat learned to make good cheese

options.A: by listening to the advice she got.
options.B: by seeing how her mother did it.
options.C: by practising at home on her own.

evidenceKeywords: helped me to slowly get better
questions.explanation: 14. A: Trong đoạn 1, Pat kể lại những lần đầu tự làm phô mai không được ngon lắm: 'but the workers told me what I was doing wrong and that helped me to slowly get better' (nhưng các công nhân đã chỉ ra những điều tôi làm sai và điều đó giúp tôi từ từ làm tốt hơn), tức là cô học qua việc lắng nghe lời khuyên.

questions.text: Where does Pat get the milk for her cheese?

options.A: from her neighbour's farm
options.B: her father helps her to buy it
options.C: her parents give it to her

evidenceKeywords: ask them when she needs more
questions.explanation: 15. C: Ở đoạn 2, bài đọc nêu: 'Her mother and father keep 100 cows on their farm. Pat can just ask them when she needs more' (Bố mẹ cô nuôi 100 con bò trên trang trại. Pat chỉ cần hỏi xin họ khi cô cần thêm), nghĩa là bố mẹ cung cấp sữa cho cô.

questions.text: Pat says winning the prize means

options.A: more people know about her family's cheese.
options.B: she can be the star of the family's new advertisements.
options.C: she was invited to visit another country.

questions.explanation: 16. A: Trong đoạn 3, Pat chia sẻ ý nghĩa của việc đạt giải thưởng: 'It's been great for helping customers find out about us' (Thật tuyệt vời vì nó giúp khách hàng biết đến chúng tôi), đồng nghĩa với việc ngày càng có nhiều người biết về phô mai của gia đình cô.

questions.text: What's the most important thing for Pat at the moment?

options.A: writing a new cookbook
options.B: being successful at school
options.C: selling cheese to a restaurant

questions.explanation: 17. B: Ở đoạn cuối, Pat khẳng định dù việc làm phô mai và đạt giải thưởng rất tuyệt, 'but doing well in my studies matters more for now' (nhưng việc học tập tốt ở trường quan trọng hơn đối với tôi vào lúc này).

questions.text: In this article, Pat is explaining

options.A: why her family started making cheese.
options.B: how to win a competition for making cheese.
options.C: how she has become part of a cheese-making business.

questions.explanation: 18. C: Xuyên suốt bài viết, tác giả miêu tả quá trình phát triển của Pat từ lúc mới làm quen với việc làm phô mai trên trang trại gia đình, đến khi dần dần tham gia vào quá trình kinh doanh (chế biến, quảng bá nhờ giải thưởng, bán sang nước ngoài, dự định viết công thức online).

title: A path across Canada
content_html:
Thirteen-year-old Kara Finch writes about The Great Trail – a footpath that goes across Canada.

The Great Trail goes from one side of Canada to the other. At 24,000 km, it is the longest trail in the world. The Great Trail project began in 1992 and took 25 years to complete. It opened in 2017, exactly 150 years after Canada became one country.

The Great Trail is actually lots of paths joined together. You can cycle, walk or horse-ride on most of it, but there are parts where the only way you can travel is by boat. And there are others where the trail is part of the highway, which doesn’t sound very safe to me.

My father and I spent a day walking along part of the Great Trail that was surprisingly busy. We were told you don’t usually see bears when there are lots of people, but we did. It was moving through the trees, quite close to us, and I felt a little afraid. ‘I don’t think it’s hungry!’ laughed my dad.

At lunchtime, we talked to an old man on the trail. Dad asked him if he thought the path was going to help Canadians learn more about their countryside. ‘Not really,’ he said. ‘We’ve always loved to be outdoors. But it will bring us tourists from around the world, which is important.’

In the future, the path will probably change, and new parts may open. We saw nothing to tell us we were on the Great Trail, which is a shame. I know there’s information on the website, but it’s good to know when you’re walking on a special path. They need to add some signs, I think.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What is Kara doing in the first paragraph?

options.A: telling tourists how long it takes to walk the Great Trail
options.B: explaining the history of the Great Trail
options.C: giving walkers advice about walking in Canada

questions.explanation: 14. B: Trong đoạn 1, Kara cung cấp các mốc thời gian như dự án bắt đầu năm 1992, mất 25 năm hoàn thành và mở cửa vào năm 2017. Những thông tin này chính là đang giải thích lịch sử (history) của con đường The Great Trail.

questions.text: What does Kara think is dangerous?

options.A: Some parts of the path are on roads.
options.B: You need a boat for parts of the path.
options.C: Bikes and horses share the same path.

questions.explanation: 15. A: Ở đoạn 2, Kara đề cập: 'there are others where the trail is part of the highway, which doesn’t sound very safe to me' (có những đoạn mà đường mòn nằm ngay trên đường cao tốc/đường bộ, điều đó nghe có vẻ không an toàn lắm đối với tôi).

questions.text: What does Kara say about the bear she saw?

options.A: It looked hungry.
options.B: It was on the path.
options.C: It walked near her.

questions.explanation: 16. C: Trong đoạn 3, Kara miêu tả con gấu đang di chuyển qua những cái cây và 'quite close to us' (khá gần với chúng tôi), đồng nghĩa với việc nó đi dạo gần cô.

questions.text: What did the old man say about the trail?

options.A: People from other countries will come to see it.
options.B: People will go into the countryside more because of it.
options.C: People from Canada will use it to see other parts of their country.

questions.explanation: 17. A: Theo đoạn 4, ông lão nhận định con đường 'will bring us tourists from around the world' (sẽ mang đến cho chúng ta khách du lịch từ khắp nơi trên thế giới), tương ứng với việc người từ các quốc gia khác sẽ đến xem.

questions.text: What does Kara say should happen next?

options.A: There should be a website about the trail.
options.B: Some parts of the trail should close.
options.C: There should be more signs along the trail.

questions.explanation: 18. C: Đoạn cuối Kara bày tỏ sự tiếc nuối khi không có gì báo hiệu họ đang ở trên con đường đặc biệt này, và đề xuất: 'They need to add some signs, I think' (Tôi nghĩ họ cần bổ sung thêm một số biển báo).

title: Callie Rice – ice dancer
content_html:
Growing up, I was in an ice-skating club, and my friends and I did lots of competitions. When I was 18, I entered a big one in Italy, and I came first. The prize was a job as an ice dancer in a big show. That’s how my career started.

One of the things I love about the shows is the beautiful clothes. The only trouble is that sometimes they’re heavy and I get very hot. Also, I have to change lots of times in one show. The people who make the clothes are very clever – it’s amazing how quickly they work!

Before the show starts, we get dressed, put our skates on, and wish each other good luck. Then, while I’m waiting to go on, I like to sit quietly by myself and think about what I’m going to do. The other skaters chat together or listen to music.

In the shows, we have to dance, sing, and also speak. For me, ice dancing is fine, but I’m not an actor. I’m always a bit scared I won’t remember the words. I’m not bothered about falling – it doesn’t usually happen. And I really enjoy joining in with all the songs.

People always ask me if I’m tired when each show finishes, but I love skating, so I never feel that way. I love listening to the people who came to watch – so excited as they leave, talking about what they’ve seen. That’s when I’m really happy I chose ice dancing as a career.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: How did Callie’s career as an ice dancer begin?

options.A: She won a competition.
options.B: She saw an advert for a job.
options.C: A friend invited her to join an ice show.

questions.explanation: 14. A: Trong đoạn 1, Callie kể cô đã tham gia một cuộc thi lớn, 'came first' (giành giải nhất) và phần thưởng chính là một công việc vũ công trên băng, mở đầu cho sự nghiệp của cô.

questions.text: What does Callie say about the clothes she wears?

options.A: Some are prettier than others.
options.B: It’s hard to skate in them.
options.C: Making them takes a long time.

questions.explanation: 15. B: Ở đoạn 2, Callie chia sẻ rắc rối duy nhất với những bộ trang phục đẹp là 'sometimes they’re heavy and I get very hot' (đôi khi chúng nặng và khiến tôi rất nóng), gây khó khăn/cản trở khi trượt.

questions.text: What does Callie do just before each show?

options.A: She talks to the other skaters.
options.B: She checks her skates are okay.
options.C: She takes a moment to be alone.

evidenceKeywords: think about what I'm going to do
questions.explanation: 16. C: Đoạn 3 nói về thói quen trước khi ra diễn, trong khi những người khác trò chuyện hay nghe nhạc thì cô 'like to sit quietly by myself' (thích ngồi yên tĩnh một mình) để suy nghĩ về bài diễn.

questions.text: What does Callie worry about?

options.A: having an accident on the ice
options.B: forgetting what she has to say
options.C: dancing and singing at the same time

evidenceKeywords: scared I won't remember the words
questions.explanation: 17. B: Trong đoạn 4, cô chia sẻ việc phải nhảy, hát và nói. Vì không phải là diễn viên nên cô 'always a bit scared I won't remember the words' (luôn hơi sợ sẽ không nhớ lời thoại).

questions.text: How does Callie usually feel after a show?

options.A: ready for a rest
options.B: glad that she’s finished
options.C: pleased about making people happy

questions.explanation: 18. C: Đoạn cuối, cô tâm sự mình không hề thấy mệt mà rất thích nghe khán giả hào hứng trò chuyện khi ra về, điều đó khiến cô 'really happy' (thực sự hạnh phúc) vì lựa chọn nghề nghiệp của mình.

title: A wonderful year
content_html:
Ben Jones talks about his year of travel before studying at university.

I always knew I wanted to spend a year travelling before university. I wanted to see the world, improve my skiing, make new friends from other countries and, more than anything, try lots of things I’ve never done before.

The first place I visited was France. I’ve always loved the French language and I wanted to study it. Then, while I was there, I became interested in French food. I took some cooking lessons at a farmhouse in the French countryside, and learned to make lots of lovely dishes.

Next, I visited Australia. My cousins live near the river in Sydney, so I had somewhere to stay. To begin with, we spent a lot of time sailing in their boat. But then I got a job in a hotel. It was great – the customers and staff were so friendly.

My last country was Japan, where I learned to become a skiing teacher. I planned to do a three-week course. But when I got there, I saw that I wasn’t actually that good, so I changed to a seven-week one. The course was amazing and afterwards I got a job with a skiing company.

The other teachers were very friendly and on our days off, we did activities like snowboarding. One weekend we even did winter camping. That was quite interesting, but it was cold, so I can’t say I slept well! Anyway, we’re all planning to meet up soon and I can’t wait.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What did Ben want to do during the year after he left school?

options.A: try some different sports
options.B: have some new experiences
options.C: meet up with old friends

questions.explanation: 14. B: Đoạn 1, Ben liệt kê nhiều việc muốn làm trong năm đi du lịch, và nhấn mạnh 'more than anything, try lots of things I’ve never done before' (trên hết là thử nhiều điều tôi chưa từng làm trước đây), tức là muốn có những trải nghiệm mới.

questions.text: Why did Ben go to France?

options.A: to do a French cooking course
options.B: to see the French countryside
options.C: to learn to speak French

questions.explanation: 15. C: Trong đoạn 2, Ben nêu rõ mục đích ban đầu đến Pháp là vì 'loved the French language and I wanted to study it' (yêu thích tiếng Pháp và muốn học nó). Việc học nấu ăn chỉ phát sinh sau khi anh đã ở đó.

questions.text: Where did Ben live when he was in Australia?

options.A: in the hotel where he worked
options.B: with members of his family
options.C: on a boat on the river

questions.explanation: 16. B: Ở đoạn 3, Ben kể 'My cousins live near the river in Sydney, so I had somewhere to stay' (Anh em họ của tôi sống gần sông ở Sydney, nên tôi có chỗ để ở), tức là anh sống cùng thành viên trong gia đình.

questions.text: Why did Ben change his course in Japan?

options.A: He heard about a better one.
options.B: He knew he needed more practice.
options.C: He wasn’t enjoying the first one.

questions.explanation: 17. B: Đoạn 4 cho biết Ben ban đầu định học khóa 3 tuần nhưng khi đến nơi thì nhận ra 'I wasn’t actually that good, so I changed to a seven-week one' (tôi thực sự không giỏi đến thế, nên đã đổi sang khóa 7 tuần), chứng tỏ anh biết mình cần luyện tập thêm.

questions.text: What does Ben say about winter camping?

options.A: It wasn’t pleasant at night.
options.B: He would love to do it again soon.
options.C: It was the most interesting thing he’s ever done.

questions.explanation: 18. A: Đoạn cuối Ben miêu tả trải nghiệm cắm trại mùa đông khá thú vị nhưng 'it was cold, so I can't say I slept well' (trời rất lạnh nên tôi không thể nói là mình đã ngủ ngon), cho thấy trải nghiệm ban đêm không mấy dễ chịu.

title: Jack Calder
content_html:
Violin player Jack Calder plays in the Australian band, Ocean Blue.

Jack Calder started playing the violin when he was ten. ‘My music teacher played and one day he asked if anyone wanted to learn. Some girls put up their hands and so did I. I didn’t have a violin, but my uncle said I could use his. The lessons were really hard at first, but playing the violin soon became important to me.’

After leaving school, Jack moved to Melbourne. For a time, he preferred listening to music to playing it. The rock music he listened to sounded very different from violin music, so he bought an electric violin, and started putting the things he liked about rock music into the music he played on his violin.

A year later, Jack met a small group of Melbourne musicians. ‘We all thought about music in the same way and started Ocean Blue together. A year later, we were playing lots of concerts, and our music was selling well. But we didn’t want this to make us different people. We didn’t want to stop being friends.’

Jack meets many people who think playing the violin is an unusual career, but he doesn’t agree with them. ‘I think it’s the best thing in the world. I guess I’m lucky that way. The internet has changed music, but when I walk into a violin shop it’s like entering another world - one where time has stopped. Someone has looked after these beautiful old instruments that are two or three hundred years old. I think that’s amazing.’


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What do we learn about Jack in the first paragraph?

options.A: He was the only person at school to play the violin.
options.B: He learned to play on an instrument that he borrowed.
options.C: He enjoyed playing the violin as soon as he started learning.

evidenceKeywords: uncle said I could use his
questions.explanation: 14. B: Trong đoạn 1, Jack kể lại: 'I didn’t have a violin, but my uncle said I could use his' (Tôi không có đàn violin, nhưng chú tôi bảo tôi có thể dùng của chú ấy), nghĩa là cậu đã học trên một cây đàn đi mượn.

questions.text: What is the writer doing in the second paragraph?

options.A: explaining why Jack thought some music was easy to play
options.B: saying why only a few people liked the music Jack played
options.C: describing how Jack changed the kind of music he played

evidenceKeywords: putting the things he liked about rock music
questions.explanation: 15. C: Đoạn 2 miêu tả việc Jack mua một cây violin điện và 'started putting the things he liked about rock music into the music he played on his violin' (bắt đầu đưa những thứ anh thích ở nhạc rock vào âm nhạc anh chơi trên violin), tức là mô tả cách anh thay đổi thể loại nhạc mình chơi.

questions.text: What does Jack say about Ocean Blue?

options.A: Nobody in the band liked travelling far to play in a concert.
options.B: The band members were interested in different kinds of music.
options.C: Everyone wanted to stay friends when the band became successful.

evidenceKeywords: didn't want to stop being friends
questions.explanation: 16. C: Ở đoạn 3, sau khi ban nhạc bán được nhiều đĩa và chơi nhiều buổi hòa nhạc, Jack chia sẻ: 'We didn’t want this to make us different people. We didn’t want to stop being friends' (Chúng tôi không muốn điều này biến chúng tôi thành những con người khác. Chúng tôi không muốn ngừng làm bạn).

questions.text: Why does Jack think he is lucky?

options.A: He meets lots of people.
options.B: He loves what he does.
options.C: He has an unusual career.

evidenceKeywords: think it's the best thing in the world
questions.explanation: 17. B: Trong đoạn cuối, đáp lại những người cho rằng chơi violin là một nghề kỳ lạ, Jack nói: 'I think it’s the best thing in the world. I guess I’m lucky that way' (Tôi nghĩ đó là điều tuyệt vời nhất trên đời. Tôi đoán mình may mắn theo cách đó), chứng tỏ anh thấy may mắn vì được làm công việc mình vô cùng yêu thích.

questions.text: Jack thinks it is a good idea

options.A: to keep some things that people used in the past.
options.B: to make more music available on the internet.
options.C: to teach more people to play an instrument.

evidenceKeywords: looked after these beautiful old instruments
two or three hundred years old
questions.explanation: 18. A: Cuối bài, Jack bày tỏ sự trân trọng đối với những nhạc cụ cổ: 'Someone has looked after these beautiful old instruments that are two or three hundred years old. I think that’s amazing' (Ai đó đã chăm sóc những nhạc cụ cũ tuyệt đẹp này từ hai ba trăm năm trước. Tôi nghĩ điều đó thật đáng kinh ngạc).

title: Joining a ballroom dancing club
content_html:
By Pippa Cartwright

When I started college, I wanted to find a club to join. One of the first ones I looked at was ballroom dancing – a type of dance you do with a partner. The people there seemed to be having a great time, and it didn’t cost much, so I decided to join.

The first week I went, I was really worried because the teacher told us that there were nineteen different dances we had to learn. But it’s been fine. When there’s a new thing to learn, he shows it to us lots of times and makes sure we’re all good at it before we do the next thing.

When I joined, I didn’t know any of the other people in the club because we all study different subjects. But it’s been a great way to meet people, and I’ve made some of my best friends in the club.

One of the reasons we learn the dances is to enter competitions. I couldn’t wait to do my first one. Before we started, I was a bit worried. But during the competition, my partner and I remembered everything about our dances. We were great. We didn’t win any prizes, but it didn’t matter – we loved it!

Joining the ballroom dancing club has been fantastic. In the past, I always did the same sports and activities, year after year, but ballroom dancing has taught me there’s nothing scary about doing something you’ve never tried before. I still do lots of sports, but now I can add ballroom dancing to my list of hobbies.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: Why did Pippa join the dance club?

options.A: She thought it looked fun.
options.B: She didn’t have to pay for it.
options.C: She didn’t like any of the other clubs.

evidenceKeywords: seemed to be having a great time
questions.explanation: 14. A: Trong đoạn 1, Pippa lý giải quyết định tham gia là do: 'The people there seemed to be having a great time' (Mọi người ở đó có vẻ đang có khoảng thời gian rất tuyệt vời/vui vẻ).

questions.text: What does Pippa say about the dance teacher?

options.A: He teaches them a new dance every week.
options.B: He often tells new members how good they are.
options.C: He repeats new things until everyone can do them.

evidenceKeywords: shows it to us lots of times
makes sure we're all good at it
questions.explanation: 15. C: Ở đoạn 2, cô nhận xét về thầy giáo: 'he shows it to us lots of times and makes sure we’re all good at it before we do the next thing' (thầy làm mẫu cho chúng tôi nhiều lần và đảm bảo tất cả chúng tôi đều thành thạo trước khi chuyển sang phần tiếp theo).

questions.text: What does Pippa say about the other club members?

options.A: She has become close to some of them.
options.B: She is on the same course as some of them.
options.C: She was friends with some of them before joining.

evidenceKeywords: made some of my best friends
questions.explanation: 16. A: Đoạn 3 ghi rõ: 'I’ve made some of my best friends in the club' (Tôi đã kết bạn được với một số người bạn thân nhất của mình trong câu lạc bộ), đồng nghĩa với việc cô đã trở nên thân thiết với họ.

questions.text: How did Pippa feel about her first dance competition?

options.A: happy to win first prize
options.B: upset that she forgot the dances
options.C: excited to take part

questions.explanation: 17. C: Trong đoạn 4, dù có chút lo lắng trước khi bắt đầu, cảm xúc chủ đạo của Pippa là 'I couldn’t wait to do my first one' (Tôi vô cùng háo hức/không thể chờ đợi để tham gia lần thi đầu tiên).

questions.text: In the final paragraph, Pippa says

options.A: ballroom dancing is her favourite hobby.
options.B: she’s learned not to be afraid to do new things.
options.C: she isn’t sure which activity to try next.

evidenceKeywords: doing something you've never tried before
questions.explanation: 18. B: Ở đoạn cuối, Pippa đúc kết: 'ballroom dancing has taught me there’s nothing scary about doing something you’ve never tried before' (khiêu vũ thể thao đã dạy tôi rằng không có gì đáng sợ khi làm một điều mình chưa từng thử trước đây).

title: My city
content_html:
Pop singer Charlotte Bond talks about living in London.

I live in the centre of London. I love it because there’s always something happening and there are people around whatever time it is. Famous people like it too – they often come here for the restaurants and shops.

I’ve lived here all my life. When I was little, I had singing lessons at a place near where I live now. I was afraid of the teacher at first, and some of the songs we did together were quite hard to learn. But she was good at what she did and I learned a lot of things that have helped me in my career.

When friends visit me now, I enjoy taking them sightseeing. You can get a bus around the city, but we prefer to walk. I’ve got a little car and I love driving, but there’s so much traffic here, and it’s hard to find parking spaces.

One building I love is the Natural History Museum. They sometimes hold parties there, and last December my band and I played at one. I’ll never forget it. When I go to exhibitions at the museum with my friends, I tell them all about that night and how amazing it was.

Soon I’ll be leaving London to go on tour with my band. We’re playing in lots of new cities and I can’t wait to explore them. We’ve sold lots of tickets, which is great. I’ll be away from my family for six months, but they’re coming to see me sing, so it’s fine.


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: What does Charlotte love about the centre of London?

options.A: It is always busy.
options.B: Famous people often visit.
options.C: The shops are very good.

evidenceKeywords: people around whatever time it is
questions.explanation: 14. A: Trong đoạn 1, Charlotte nói cô yêu nơi này vì 'there’s always something happening and there are people around whatever time it is' (luôn có sự kiện diễn ra và luôn có người xung quanh bất kể giờ giấc), thể hiện sự bận rộn, nhộn nhịp.

questions.text: How does Charlotte feel about the singing lessons she had?

options.A: She’s surprised she can remember them.
options.B: She’s sorry she didn’t try harder.
options.C: She’s glad she did them.

questions.explanation: 15. C: Ở đoạn 2, dù lúc đầu sợ giáo viên và bài hát khó, cô nhận định: 'I learned a lot of things that have helped me in my career' (tôi đã học được rất nhiều điều giúp ích cho sự nghiệp của mình), cho thấy sự biết ơn/hài lòng vì đã tham gia các lớp học đó.

questions.text: What does Charlotte think is the best way to see the city?

options.A: by car
options.B: on foot
options.C: by bus

questions.explanation: 16. B: Đoạn 3 có nêu khi dẫn bạn bè đi ngắm cảnh, dù có thể đi xe buýt nhưng 'we prefer to walk' (chúng tôi thích đi bộ hơn).

questions.text: Why does Charlotte love the Natural History Museum?

options.A: She had a special experience there.
options.B: She thinks the building is beautiful.
options.C: She enjoys visiting the exhibitions.

evidenceKeywords: band and I played at one
questions.explanation: 17. A: Trong đoạn 4, lý do cô yêu tòa nhà này là vì ban nhạc của cô từng biểu diễn tại một bữa tiệc ở đó: 'I’ll never forget it... tell them all about that night and how amazing it was' (Tôi sẽ không bao giờ quên... kể cho họ nghe về đêm đó tuyệt vời như thế nào).

questions.text: What does Charlotte say about going on tour with her band?

options.A: She hopes lots of people will buy tickets for her shows.
options.B: She feels excited about seeing new places.
options.C: She’s worried she’ll miss her family.

questions.explanation: 18. B: Ở đoạn cuối, Charlotte háo hức chia sẻ: 'We’re playing in lots of new cities and I can’t wait to explore them' (Chúng tôi sẽ biểu diễn ở nhiều thành phố mới và tôi vô cùng háo hức/không thể chờ đợi để khám phá chúng).

title: Travel writer
content_html:
Tim Greenwood lives in Oakland, USA. He has written about Cambodia, Thailand and India. At the moment he is writing a book about Nepal.

Tim, how did you start travelling?
We never travelled as a family, but when I was a teenager I saw movies like Lawrence of Arabia and they made me want to travel. I left college at the age of 21 and went to Europe alone. That trip didn’t go well – I was too young and didn’t know how to look after myself.

How did you start writing?
I’ve loved writing since I was ten – I’ve never wanted any other career. At 21, I wrote for my weekly college paper and then started writing travel articles. At first, none of the travel magazines I sent them to wanted to buy them, but that slowly changed. It took two or three years, I guess.

What do you find difficult about writing?
It is easy to spend all my time travelling and then not have time to open up my laptop and work! Also, it’s hard to earn enough money. I’ll never stop writing, but one day I may have to do a few hours a week teaching just to pay the bills.

What’s the best thing about being a travel writer?
I get letters from young people who’ve read my books and articles and enjoy my work. I just love that!


Compiled & Digitized by Elaine Nguyễn

questions.text: As a teenager, Tim

options.A: went on trips with his parents.
options.B: became interested in seeing the world.
options.C: spent too much time watching TV.

questions.explanation: 14. B: Trong phần trả lời đầu tiên, Tim kể: 'when I was a teenager I saw movies like Lawrence of Arabia and they made me want to travel' (khi còn là thanh thiếu niên, tôi xem những bộ phim... và chúng khiến tôi muốn đi du lịch/khám phá thế giới).

questions.text: What does Tim say about his trip to Europe?

options.A: He didn’t have time to see everything.
options.B: It was more fun than college.
options.C: It was not a great success.

evidenceKeywords: didn't know how to look after myself
questions.explanation: 15. C: Tim mô tả chuyến đi Châu Âu của mình: 'That trip didn’t go well – I was too young and didn’t know how to look after myself' (Chuyến đi đó không suôn sẻ - tôi còn quá trẻ và không biết cách tự chăm sóc bản thân), tức là nó không thành công lắm.

questions.text: When Tim was 21, he couldn’t

options.A: travel as much as he wanted to.
options.B: decide what to write about.
options.C: sell many of his articles.

questions.explanation: 16. C: Ở phần hỏi đáp thứ hai, Tim chia sẻ thời điểm bắt đầu viết lúc 21 tuổi: 'none of the travel magazines I sent them to wanted to buy them' (không một tạp chí du lịch nào tôi gửi bài muốn mua chúng), đồng nghĩa với việc anh không bán được nhiều bài viết.

questions.text: In the future, Tim thinks he might

options.A: do some extra work.
options.B: earn more from writing.
options.C: change his job.

evidenceKeywords: may have to do a few hours a week teaching
questions.explanation: 17. A: Trong đoạn thứ ba, do khó khăn về thu nhập, Tim dự tính: 'one day I may have to do a few hours a week teaching just to pay the bills' (một ngày nào đó tôi có thể phải đi dạy vài giờ một tuần chỉ để trang trải chi phí), tương đương với việc làm thêm một công việc khác.

questions.text: What does Tim like about being a travel writer?

options.A: hearing from his fans
options.B: giving advice to people
options.C: meeting other young writers

questions.explanation: 18. A: Ở câu hỏi cuối, Tim khẳng định điều tuyệt vời nhất là: 'I get letters from young people who’ve read my books and articles and enjoy my work. I just love that!' (Tôi nhận được thư từ những người trẻ đọc và yêu thích tác phẩm của tôi. Tôi rất thích điều đó!), nghĩa là anh thích được nghe phản hồi từ người hâm mộ.
